Petition For Removal Of David Lee As Personal Representative And An Order For The Appointment Of Successor Personal Representative
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Spalding for and with Nelson Lee, Petitioner and Proposed Successor Personal Representative (via Zoom).

Both Mr. Spalding and Mr. Lee confirmed no contact had been received from David Lee. The court inquired as to whether the estate has any assets. Mr. Spalding reported that the estate's primary asset is a commercial condominium unit in Hong Kong. Counsel was unable to provide an estimate as to the time needed to close the estate due to the constraints of the Hong Kong proceedings.

The court took the petition for removal of David Lee as Personal Representative under submission with intent to grant and appoint Nelson Lee as the Successor Personal Representative. The status on close of estate and the petition for probate of will were continued to April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Order to Show Cause and Order to Appear hearings were taken off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

POST-SCRIPT:

1/21/2025; 4:45 PM

After conclusion of the hearing, Judge Murphy reconsidered continuing the hearing on the Petition for Probate of Will. The petition was granted, and a Status on Close of Estate was set for January 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Therefore, the hearing on Status of Close that had previously been continued to 4/1/2025 has been re-set to 1/20/2026, and the 4/1/2025 hearing on Probate of Will is off calendar.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Continued To Apr-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of David Lee As Personal Representative And An Order For The Appointment Of Successor Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Continued To Jan-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Spalding for Nelson Lee, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The court noted procedural deficiencies in Mr. Spalding's petition. Mr. Spalding stated that he recently filed an errata/declaration to address the missing page of the initial filing and verified Nelson Lee's status as trustee and beneficiary.

Mr. Spalding confirmed no contact has been made with David Lee and no evidence of his relocation or passing has been obtained.

The court ordered that David Lee is suspended as administrator. The court will send notice of suspension to the surety for the existing bond of $10,000. An Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause will be issued to David Lee to appear on January 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to show cause why he should not be removed as administrator for his failure to appear at today's hearing and his failure to comply with the prior Order to Show Cause. The petitions are also continued to be heard on the same date.

Mr. Spalding was instructed by the court to refile the petitions in a single complete package and to serve all appropriate parties with the updated petitions and include the Notice of Remote Appearance.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Oct-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Spalding for Nelson Lee.

Mr. Spalding stated that he filed a petition on behalf of Nelson, that Mr. Seher filed a competing petition on behalf of David and that the Court decided to appoint David. Mr. Spalding also stated that he has heard nothing, that he came back because he is concerned that nothing is happening, and that he is wondering if David should be removed and Nelson be appointed. The Court noted that there is no petition to do that. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator David Lee and the Attorney Alan Seher to appear on October 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan Seher for David Lee (Petitioner).
Michael Spalding for Nelson Lee (Petitioner).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to their Clients being entitled to serve as Executor/Administrator, read language in the will, spoke of the testator's intent and her relationship with all of the Children (including the stepchildren), and reported that the only asset is an interest in a probate estate in Hong Kong and that the stepchildren will receive a small portion under Hong Kong law. The Court stated that it seems the intent was to have all of the Children including the stepchildren decide on who the Executor is. The Court granted David Lee's Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). The amount of bond is to be determined by the Court. Status on close of estate is set for September 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.
